creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
Hi,

when I delete large amount of data on ZFS, for example 5 GB (which is not that large anyway) the rm(1) command does it 'instantly' and the disk start to work like crazy, it lasts for several minutes and disk is very saturated with I/O while this 'background' removal is in progress.

How one can limit or slow down or throttle that background files removal in ZFS?

Is there some sysctl(8) for that?

Thanks in advance.
